resources.string:
Your bike's dash speaks CFMoto's MotoPlay protocol, which expects the CFMoto app. This app pretends to be that app, and feeds it Android Auto instead.

What happens when you tap Connect:

• The phone runs Android Auto against itself, and captures what it draws.
• That picture is re-encoded for the dash's panel and sent over the bike's own Wi-Fi.
• The dash shows it. Maps, Waze and Spotify all just work.

First run, once:

1. On the dash, open MotoPlay so the QR code appears, then scan it here. The QR carries the Wi-Fi name and password plus the model ID that picks your screen profile — it's saved, so you never scan again.
2. Android Auto itself must be installed and set up, with "Add new cars to Android Auto" enabled.

Every run after that: ignition on, open this app, done — it reconnects on its own.

Driving the dash: there's no touchscreen, so use the knob and D-pad on the Control tab, or just type a destination here and let Maps do the rest. Turn on "Bike buttons drive Android Auto" in Settings and the handlebar volume buttons become the knob.

If the dash stays black: kill the app, ignition off and on, and try again — the dash only hands out one projection session at a time.
